.ddx/executions/20260504T222006-82844da2/prompt.md:5:The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads

2. README sections of each refactor child (C4, C5, C6, C7, C8, C9, C11, C12, C13) cite the principle(s) the child enforces. Cross-link in those bead descriptions added via ddx bead update.
3. CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md updated with one-line pointers: 'See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.'
4. cd cli && go test ./... still green (no code change; sanity check that nothing broke).
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
6. Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].

.ddx/executions/20260505T074136-36b65cad/prompt.md:5:The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS
- Each refactor child (ddx-c8f79963 C5, ddx-06eb05d8 C7, ddx-9228a484 C9, ddx-848069a3 C8, ddx-b669bb9f C6, ddx-c670ef0a C12) gets an AC line citing the principle(s) it enforces
- Bead-quality audit applies P7 to ~20 existing open beads

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and supports P7 / bead-quality.

.ddx/executions/20260505T051503-52c98a2f/prompt.md:79:+    "title": "escalation: wire ladder into executor closure; drop tier→profile mutation",
.ddx/executions/20260505T051503-52c98a2f/prompt.md:80:+    "description": "PROBLEM
The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) was added in S10_2 but has no production caller in execute_bead_loop.go. Each bead currently gets exactly one singleTierAttempt without any rung escalation on substantive failure. The existing tier→profile mutation in the loop is an ad-hoc workaround that should be replaced by the ladder.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: singleTierAttempt is called exactly once per bead iteration; there is no loop over ladder rungs.
- cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30: Ladder.Next is defined but has no call site in execute_bead_loop.go or any other production file (confirmed by dead-code audit ddx-09d2990c).
- The existing tier→profile mutation (a manual MinPower bump) in the executor closure is the only escalation behavior; it is not driven by the ladder contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Replace the single singleTierAttempt call in execute_bead_loop.go with a real loop that:
  1. Calls singleTierAttempt with current rung's MinPower.
  2. On substantive failure (non-park, non-budget-exceeded), calls ladder.Next to get next rung.
  3. Terminates when ladder.Next returns no further rungs or a stop condition triggers.
- Remove the existing tier→profile mutation; ladder.Next drives all MinPower escalation.
- Pass Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='<bead_id>:<attempt_id>' on every ExecuteRequest.

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the ladder definition or rung configuration (S10_2 owns that).
- Cost-cap relocation (belongs to C9 / ddx-9228a484).",
.ddx/executions/20260505T051503-52c98a2f/prompt.md:81:+    "acceptance": "1. execute_bead_loop.go uses escalation.Ladder.Next on substantive failure (confirmed call site in production code, not just defined).
2. Existing tier→profile mutation removed from execute_bead_loop.go.
3. Every ExecuteRequest carries Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='<bead_id>:<attempt_id>'.
4. TestEscalationLadder_WiredIntoExecutor verifies ladder.Next is called when singleTierAttempt returns a substantive failure.
5. TestEscalationLadder_StopsAtFinalRung verifies loop terminates when ladder.Next exhausts rungs.
6. Tests in cli/internal/agent/zero_config_escalation_integration_test.go (or new test file) exercise actual escalation, not just primitives.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/manifest.json:13:    "description": "PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.
- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep order.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T200645-f1713f26/prompt.md:5:PROBLEM
Operators have no way to dispatch a SPECIFIC bead through the production execution machinery (claim → try.Attempt → land → review). Today's surfaces:
- 'ddx work' drains by picker priority — can't force a specific bead
- 'ddx work --once' drains the top of the queue once — still picker-controlled
- 'ddx agent run --prompt' invokes the agent but doesn't claim, doesn't merge, doesn't write to bead event log

This blocks: (1) deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution, (2) the 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) which needs a way to target one fixture bead at a time, (3) operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain.

ROOT CAUSE
'ddx try' was originally bundled into refactor child C13 (ddx-387a0178) along with the file rename. C13 is sequenced AFTER C7 / C9 because the rename touches execute_bead_loop.go which those children also touch. But 'ddx try' itself is ADDITIVE — a new CLI subcommand that delegates to existing machinery — and doesn't conflict with the refactor children. Splitting 'ddx try' out lets it ship FIRST and unblocks operator-controlled execution for the entire rest of the refactor work.

Code today (relevant entry points):
- cli/cmd/work.go: 'ddx work' command implementation (calls into agent.ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime)
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: 'ddx agent execute-loop' (the underlying loop runner)
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go: ExecuteBeadWorker.Run + nextCandidate
- cli/internal/bead/store.go:1246 ReadyExecution + Claim

PROPOSED FIX
Add a new top-level command at cli/cmd/try.go:

  ddx try <bead-id> [--harness=<name>] [--model=<name>] [--profile=<name>]

Behavior:
1. Look up the bead by ID via the bead store; fail-fast with a clear message if not found.
2. Verify the bead is in a claimable state (status open OR stale-in_progress per existing semantics); fail-fast if status closed/cancelled OR if it has unmet dependencies.
3. Force-target this single bead. Two implementation strategies (pick simpler):
   STRATEGY A (thin wrapper): construct an ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime with --once + a custom nextCandidate that returns ONLY this bead-id, skipping the picker. Call ExecuteBeadWorker.Run.
   STRATEGY B (direct invocation): claim the bead, build the runtime config, call try.Attempt directly (or whatever the underlying single-bead execution function is today). Skip the loop.
   Recommend STRATEGY A — reuses the existing claim + executor + outcome paths; smaller diff; less risk of behavior divergence.
4. Honor --harness / --model / --profile / --opaque-passthrough exactly the way 'ddx work' does (same flag plumbing).
5. Exit with the bead's terminal status (success → 0; preserved/no_changes/blocked → non-zero with structured stderr).

NON-SCOPE
- File rename of execute_bead_loop.go (still owned by C13)
- Refactor of execute-bead surface into try/work packages (refactor epic)
- Multi-bead try (use 'ddx work' for that)
- Server-side targeting (a future enhancement; v1 is local CLI only)
- Auto-discovery of dependencies (operator must use 'ddx bead ready' first)

INTERSECTION
- C13 (ddx-387a0178) currently includes 'ADD ddx try CLI command' in its scope. After this bead lands, amend C13 to drop that line.
- 3-path comparison study (ddx-31f745cd) Path A uses 'ddx try' as the production-path executor; this bead unblocks that study.
- Refactor children Wave 2 dispatch will use 'ddx try' (codex harness) to deterministically dispatch each child in dep order.

.ddx/executions/20260503T021724-8a33226e/manifest.json:13:    "description": "The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had execute-loop-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. Verify and align.",

.ddx/executions/20260503T021424-dac100b6/manifest.json:13:    "description": "Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

Proposed architectural shift: WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API.

DESIGN PROPOSALS (this TD chooses among them)

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

INTEGRATION WITH IN-FLIGHT WORK

This TD intersects:
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): the spec becomes the registration payload — single struct still applies
- ddx-5cb6e6cd refactor epic: the work package boundary aligns with worker autonomy; C9 (StopCondition) becomes worker-side state
- ddx-4c51d33e (cross-project leak): workers identify their project at registration; server enforces project boundaries via session token
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): becomes natural — worker stays alive, polls for work, doesn't exit on empty queue

IMPLEMENTATION OUTLINE (post-TD)
1. Define API contract + handlers (cli/internal/server/worker_api.go)
2. Add worker client (cli/internal/agent/work/client.go) implementing the API
3. Add in-process API implementation for --local mode (cli/internal/agent/work/local_api.go)
4. Migrate execute_bead_loop / drain_loop to consume the API instead of direct store access
5. Migrate server worker spawn to register-then-poll instead of inline-execution

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS TD
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or new FEAT) describing the worker contract
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups

DEPS
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads",

.ddx/executions/20260502T192934-7a9b3770/result.json:9:  "no_changes_rationale": "# no_changes_rationale — ddx-aede917d

## Outcome
no_changes — blocked on upstream Fizeau release.

## Why this attempt produced no commit

This bead is explicitly marked `blocked-on-upstream:agent-63bf58a6` in
its labels and states in its description:

  > This bead is downstream of Fizeau agent-63bf58a6 — depends on its
  > tagged release. Until that lands, DDx falls back to today's
  > behavior (failing beads on quota errors).

Implementing AC1–AC9 requires a typed `NoViableProviderForNow` error
(with `retry_after` and `exhausted_providers` fields) returned from
the Fizeau dispatcher. The drain loop must be able to type-assert this
error to distinguish quota pause from `execution_failed`.

## Verification that the upstream is not yet released

1. cli/go.mod pins `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau v0.10.0`.
2. `grep -rn NoViableProviderForNow` in
   `~/go/pkg/mod/github.com/!document!driven!d!x/fizeau@v0.10.0/`
   returns no Go-source matches. The only mention is inside the
   embedded `.ddx/beads.jsonl`, where Fizeau bead `agent-63bf58a6` is
   still `status:"open"`.
3. No tagged Fizeau release containing the new error type is available
   to upgrade to.

## Why we cannot proceed via a `replace` directive

User-pinned policy (memory: feedback_no_local_replace) — DDx must
always import properly tagged agent releases; no `go.mod replace`,
even temporarily. Implementing this bead against an in-tree or
file-replace Fizeau would violate that rule and produce a commit that
cannot be merged.

## What a follow-up needs

1. Fizeau ships a tagged release (≥ v0.11.0 or a dedicated tag) that
   exports `NoViableProviderForNow` with `RetryAfter time.Time` and
   `ExhaustedProviders []string`.
2. `cli/go.mod` is bumped to that release.
3. Then implement, in the drain loop (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go
   and cli/cmd/agent_execute_loop*.go):
   - Type-assert the dispatcher error; on `*NoViableProviderForNow`,
     do NOT mark `execution_failed`. Release the claim (worker-state
     transition only — bead status unchanged per
     TD-031 §8.3 QuotaPauseContract).
   - Append `unclaimed` to the bead's event stream and append
     `drain-paused-quota` to the worker-scoped event log with
     canonical name from TD-031 §8.3.
   - Sleep until `min(retry_after, now+--quota-pause-max)`; re-probe
     by attempting the next ready bead. Backoff if still
     `NoViableProviderForNow`.
   - If `retry_after > now + --quota-pause-max`, exit with operator
     message: `"quota exhausted, expected recovery <ts>; relaunch
     when ready"`.
   - Add `--quota-pause-max` flag (default 30m) to `ddx agent
     execute-loop` and `ddx work`.
4. Surface `drain-paused-quota` worker events in
   `cli/internal/server/workers.go` so the GraphQL workers list shows
   paused workers.
5. New test (cli/cmd or cli/internal/agent): spawn 2 workers, simulate
   one provider quota-exhausting, assert claim released and sibling
   picks the bead up.
6. Confirm `schema_compat_test.go` round-trip still passes (no schema
   change is needed — TD-031 §8.3 is explicit that this is a
   worker-state concern only).

## What is done

Nothing committed. The bead remains executable as soon as the
upstream tag is published; this rationale documents the block so the
post-merge reviewer does not flag it as `no_evidence_produced`.",

.ddx/executions/20260428T223713-1faefc18/result.json:9:  "no_changes_rationale": "Bead: ddx-88e91fdb — Add chat interface to project home page

Disposition: no commit this pass; recommend decomposing into 4–5 smaller beads.

(a) What is done in this pass
- Read the bead description and 12 acceptance criteria.
- Mapped the existing surface area:
  - GraphQL schema at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql with gqlgen-generated resolvers; subscription pattern established in resolver_sub_worker.go / resolver_sub_bead.go / resolver_sub_exec.go (buffered channel + ctx-cancel goroutine).
  - Frontend GraphQL via graphql-request (queries) + graphql-ws (subscriptions) — see cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/gql/{client.ts,subscriptions.ts}. No Houdini.
  - Project home page at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/+page.svelte (queue summary + 3 action cards). This is the host surface for the chat pane.
  - Sessions index at cli/internal/agent/session_index.go, sharded JSONL under .ddx/sessions/ (monthly). agentSessions GraphQL query already exists. This is the linkage target for AC5.
  - Agent execution entry at cli/internal/agent/service_run.go (RunWithConfigViaService → executeOnService → records a session-index row). Streaming today is per-harness; the execute-loop streams events via worker progress, not per-prompt chunks.
  - Playwright e2e pattern in cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/**/*.e2e.ts uses page.route('/graphql') interception with fixture data; no live backend.

(b) What is blocking a one-pass implementation
The bead bundles a feature that fans out across at least 5 independently-reviewable surfaces:

  1. Storage layer (.ddx/chat/<thread-id>/{manifest.json,messages.jsonl}) — new Go package with tests, append-only invariants, project/operator scoping.
  2. GraphQL schema + resolvers — chatThreads/chatThread/chatMessages queries, chatThreadCreate/chatSend mutations, chatStream subscription. Requires schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regeneration of generated.go, models.go updates, four new resolver files, integration tests.
  3. service.Execute integration — chatSend must dispatch a session and stream chunks + tool calls back over the chatStream subscription. Today service.Execute is request/response with a non-streaming Result; per-chunk streaming over the subscription bus is new infrastructure. Either: (i) add a streaming variant of executeOnService, or (ii) tail the session's evidence stream and re-emit. Both have non-trivial design choices that belong in the design note before code lands.
  4. Frontend — chat pane component, thread list, harness/profile picker, streaming render, tool-call inline rendering, Stop button (AC7), cumulative cost header (AC5/AC8). Real-time updates via subscribeChatMessages helper. Roughly parallel in size to the workers detail page.
  5. Auth posture middleware (AC9) — even as a "single-operator localhost / tsnet for v1" stance, the design note must commit to a posture and a route-gate stub must exist so future auth is schema-local. The bead description flags this as load-bearing for the "remote ops" framing.
  6. Design note in docs/helix/02-design/ answering the six subsidiary questions (persistence scope, harness selection, cost accountability, tool-execution scope, concurrency, auth). The bead's AC1 makes this a hard prerequisite — the implementation PR is supposed to *link* to a landed design note. The note must precede or accompany the implementation; it cannot be a postscript.
  7. Playwright e2e (AC10) — five-step scenario including two browser contexts on the same thread, cancel mid-flight, streamed chunk ordering. Mocking the GraphQL subscription side of this is non-trivial; the existing e2e suite mocks queries via page.route but does not exercise graphql-ws subscriptions.

A single execute-bead commit covering all of the above would either (i) be partial across most of those surfaces and red, or (ii) take many hours of focused work and produce a sprawling diff that the review gate cannot meaningfully verify in one pass. Both outcomes are worse than a clean decomposition.

Additional concrete unknowns the design note must resolve before code can land cleanly:
- Is the chatStream subscription chunk-level (assistant text deltas) or event-level (one event per tool call + one final assistant message)? This determines whether service.Execute needs a new streaming surface or can be wrapped post-hoc.
- For multi-operator on the same thread (AC6), is the second operator a read-only observer or an equal participant who can also send? The bead defaults to "two browsers on the same thread see each other within 2s" which implies multi-participant — but the cost-accountability and session-attribution model needs a per-message author identity that today's auth posture (single-operator) does not surface.
- Does chatSend run in the project worktree or a per-thread worktree? Bead suggests "same as ddx agent run" as the default, but if two threads chatSend concurrently they will race the worktree. This argues for per-thread worktree, which is a real cost (disk, fs setup) and belongs in the design note.

(c) What a follow-up attempt would need

Recommend splitting this bead into the following sub-beads, executed in order:

  Sub-bead 1: Design note (docs/helix/02-design/chat-interface.md)
    - Picks Option A vs B vs C vs D and locks it.
    - Answers the six subsidiary questions.
    - Specifies the message/thread schema (manifest.json shape, messages.jsonl entry shape).
    - Specifies whether chatStream is chunk-level or event-level.
    - Specifies worktree scoping per thread.
    - Specifies auth posture for v1 and the gap to broader deployment.
    Size: 1 pass, design only. No code.

  Sub-bead 2: Chat thread storage package (cli/internal/chat/)
    - New Go package: ChatThread, ChatMessage, AppendMessage, LoadThread, ListThreads.
    - Append-only messages.jsonl invariants, atomic manifest writes.
    - Unit tests including concurrent append safety.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 3: GraphQL surface (schema + resolvers + integration tests)
    - schema.graphql edits, gqlgen regen, four resolver files, integration tests.
    - chatSend dispatches via service.Execute and writes the assistant turn as a single message once the session completes (event-level streaming, simplest first cut). Chunk-level streaming is a follow-up.
    - chatStream subscription emits message-created events.
    - Session linkage via SessionIndexEntry.id reference on each assistant message.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 4: Frontend chat pane
    - New ChatPane.svelte component, thread list, message list, input box, harness/profile picker.
    - Mounted on project home page below the actions section.
    - subscribeChatMessages helper in src/lib/gql/subscriptions.ts.
    - Cost rollup in thread header. Stop button calls workerStop on the in-flight session's worker.
    Size: 1 pass.

  Sub-bead 5: Playwright e2e + auth posture middleware
    - Two-context test, cancel test, streaming chunk test (with mocked graphql-ws server).
    - Auth middleware gate stub on chat routes.
    Size: 1 pass.

The bead's AC11 (depends-on ddx-2513f08c, ddx-2ceb02fa) was not verified in this pass. Sub-bead 1 should re-verify those deps before locking the design.

Recommendation: re-queue this bead as a tracking parent and create the five sub-beads above. The current bead description is unusually thorough and most of its content can be lifted verbatim into the design-note sub-bead's body.",

Mapping from AC items to implementation at base:

AC1 (per-card in-flight tracking):
  cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/plugins/+page.svelte

  inFlightActions, dispatchSnapshots, workerFailures.
  lines 115-117: isBusy(name) gate.
  lines 458-477: Install/Update buttons disabled while busy.

AC2 (worker-driven refresh):
  +page.svelte lines 225-238 (subscribeToWorker), 240-258 (handleWorkerTerminal).
  On non-failure terminal phase, refreshPlugins() runs, then clearInFlight(name).

AC3 (fallback polling):
  +page.svelte lines 190-223. scheduleFallbackPolling sets a 30s timer
  (configurable via __ddxPluginFallbackDelayMs). When it fires without a
  terminal event, startFallbackPolling polls every 2s until the snapshot
  changes. Playwright test at e2e/plugins.spec.ts:349 ("plugin update falls
  back to polling when worker stream drops terminal event") exercises this.

  label replaces the status badge while workerId && action are set. Button

  on failure phases; renders red AlertCircle badge with link to worker page
  and title "Update failed"/"Install failed". handleWorkerTerminal (line 247)
  returns early on failure phases without calling refreshPlugins, preserving
  pre-dispatch state. dispatchError remains for synchronous mutation errors
  only (lines 287-289, 338-343).

AC6 (concurrent dispatch):

2. README sections of each refactor child (C4, C5, C6, C7, C8, C9, C11, C12, C13) cite the principle(s) the child enforces. Cross-link in those bead descriptions added via ddx bead update.

Conventional commit ending [<this-bead-id>].","closing_commit_sha":"baf5f748920779c3379712225341fe58ec9be46c","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.651384066Z","description":"The ddx try / ddx work execution machinery has 9 fail-prone layers (routing pre-flight, cooldown classification, claim CAS, worktree spawn, subprocess lifecycle, outcome adjudication, retry policy, land coordination, review). Today most layers fail-CLOSED — when a layer's specific check rejects, the layer wedges the entire pipeline. The auto-routing rejection bug fixed at commit 3b4f5d58 demonstrated this: one preflight check went strict and the queue locked up for hours.

The 7 principles below capture what each layer should do instead. They are documentation, not code — referenced by every refactor child's AC and by future bead-authoring guidance.

PRINCIPLE STATEMENTS

P1: FAIL-OPEN at every machinery layer. When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event — it does not wedge the pipeline. The auto-routing fix (workers.go:803, commit 3b4f5d58) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

P2: SINGLE RESPONSIBILITY per layer. Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (fizeau owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (picker owns); etc. Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

P3: OBSERVABLE DEGRADATION. Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

P4: BOUNDED BLAST RADIUS. A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. Stay-alive fix at commit 41cb762e established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend to all layers.

P5: OPERATOR-VISIBLE STATE. Worker reports current state (idle, claiming, executing, reviewing, blocked-on-X) at all times. No '8 hours running, 0 attempts' mystery state. ADR-022 rev 5 §Probe + freshness state model defines the worker side; UI workers panel surfaces it.

P6: AUTO-RETRY ONLY FOR TRANSIENT CLASSES. Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race → no cooldown, return to ready. Existing code: shouldSuppressNoProgress at execute_bead_loop.go:1545 already respects Disrupted (commit 47d8054e).

P7: BEAD = PROMPT. A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked. If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Code changes (this bead is doc-only)
- Per-layer reliability bead-quality fixes (separate refactor children)

INTERSECTIONS

Reviewed and approved by user before any implementation beads are filed from the roadmap.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:14:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"4025192","closing_commit_sha":"fabf513096b522e921020e323ef1bf4a9cf36afa","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:43:27.404796076Z","description":"Operator-observed gap: when ddx-server restarts, in-flight worker subprocesses lose their connection to the orchestrator. The current model has two execution paths (--local vs server-spawned) that diverge in lifecycle management, configuration plumbing (the dropped-flags class — see ddx-29058e2a), and observability (server can see only what it spawned).

PROPOSED ARCHITECTURAL SHIFT
WORKERS ARE LONG-LIVED PROCESSES THAT PHONE HOME TO THE SERVER VIA API. --local mode collapses to "worker registers with itself" using an in-process implementation of the same API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

THE ADR EVALUATES AND CHOOSES AMONG

PROPOSAL A — Server orchestrates, workers are long-lived clients
- Worker subprocess starts (either via `ddx work` CLI or via server-spawned)
- Worker registers with server (via /api/workers/register, gets worker-id + heartbeat token)
- Worker polls /api/workers/<id>/next-bead via long-poll or websocket; receives bead spec from server
- Worker reports progress via /api/workers/<id>/event {kind, body}
- Worker reports completion via /api/workers/<id>/result {bead-id, status, evidence}
- If server restarts: worker's heartbeat fails → worker enters "disconnected" state, finishes current bead, reconnects on server return
- If worker dies: server's heartbeat-timeout reclaims the bead

PROPOSAL B — Server is optional sidecar, worker is autonomous
- Worker runs independently; no server required
- If a server is reachable (via .ddx/server.addr), worker emits events to it best-effort
- Server is a passive observer, not an orchestrator
- Restart-safe by construction (worker doesn't depend on server)
- Loses: server-driven label filters, dynamic priority overrides, centralized cancel

PROPOSAL C — Hybrid: workers are autonomous executors but yield orchestration to server when present
- Worker checks for .ddx/server.addr at startup
- If present: registers as managed; receives bead assignments from server's queue
- If absent or unreachable: runs autonomously using local bead store
- Server restart: managed workers detect via heartbeat failure; gracefully complete current bead, then either retry registration OR fall back to autonomous mode (configurable)

UNIFIED API CONTRACT (all proposals)

POST /api/workers/register { project_root, harness, model_pref, label_filter, capabilities[] } → { worker_id, session_token, heartbeat_interval_ms }
POST /api/workers/<id>/heartbeat { state: idle|claiming|executing|reviewing } → { server_command: continue|pause|drain|terminate }
GET  /api/workers/<id>/next-bead → { bead, attempt_id, base_rev } | { wait_for_seconds: N }
POST /api/workers/<id>/event { kind, body, bead_id } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/result { bead_id, attempt_id, status, evidence_dir, commit_sha } → 204
POST /api/workers/<id>/disconnect → 204 (graceful)

The existing --local mode collapses to: a worker that registers with itself (no server) using an in-process implementation of the API. Same code path, same flags, same lifecycle.

OUTSTANDING BEADS THIS ADR MUST RECONCILE (overlap analysis required in the ADR output)

ABSORBED (implementation subsumed by the new architecture; ADR explicitly closes these as superseded once roadmap lands):
- ddx-29058e2a (ExecuteLoopSpec unification): becomes the API request/response payload — the spec IS the wire format. Refactor concern dissolves.
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): ALREADY FIXED at commit 41cb762e via flat 30s poll-interval default + attempted-map reset. Whether the new architecture preserves these tactical fixes or replaces them via API-driven semantics needs an ADR decision.
- ddx-cfedee8e (escalation: wire ladder): the ladder becomes server-side orchestration logic, not loop-body logic. ADR must specify where ladder lives in the new shape.

CONFLICTS WITH IN-FLIGHT REFACTOR (refactor epic ddx-5cb6e6cd children) — ADR must resolve sequencing:
- ddx-c8f79963 (C5: no_changes adjudication move into try.Attempt): refactors the SAME code that the API transition refactors. Either land C5 first then transition, OR fold C5 into the transition.
- ddx-06eb05d8 (C7: uniform Guard contract + delete attempted/hookFailed maps): same surface; same choice.
- ddx-848069a3 (C8: routing preflight to Drain startup): related to "server-side preflight" decision in API design.
- ddx-9228a484 (C9: StopCondition + cost-cap, absorbs ddx-cfedee8e and ddx-dc157075): heavy overlap. ADR must decide whether StopCondition becomes a server-side concept, a worker-side concept, or both.
- ddx-387a0178 (C13: file/identifier rename + ddx try CLI command): the ADR's new package layout (cli/internal/agent/work/) may conflict with C13's rename plan. Coordinate.
- ddx-1d867ec1 (rename execute_bead_loop.go): same as C13.

PARALLEL-SAFE (no conflict; ADR notes them but doesn't reshape):
- ddx-5b3e57f4 (cooldown: distinguish disrupted from gave-up): the API contract makes disruption naturally observable (server sees worker disconnect → marks Disrupted). But the cooldown logic itself is independent. ADR should call out that the API design enables cleaner Disrupted classification.
- ddx-1e516bc9 (fizeau Execute upstream gap): independent of API design.
- ddx-4c51d33e LAYER 1, ddx-055e8d32 LAYER 2, ddx-5ae050dc LAYER 3 (GraphQL project scoping): all in cli/internal/server/graphql/. Independent of worker API.
- ddx-9d55601f (picker bug): already fixed.
- ddx-a946c744 + ddx-aee651ec + ddx-3e60fd84 + ddx-83440482 (REACH/checks): hook into pre_merge gate; needs to integrate with the new worker contract but doesn't block ADR.
- All Story 6/14/15/16 frontend + backend feature beads: independent.

SPEC UPDATES THIS ADR DRIVES (the ADR roadmap must include these as deliverables)

- New ADR document: docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- FEAT-006 (Agent Service) — major amendment: worker API contract section, --local-collapses-to-API explanation, deprecation of direct execution paths
- FEAT-010 (Executions) — amendment: worker lifecycle now spans across server restarts; "execution" becomes a bead-attempt regardless of which worker performs it
- SD-024 (Resolved Config / CLIOverrides) — possible amendment: if ResolvedConfig becomes part of the API request payload, SD-024 reshapes
- TD-030 (Backend interface + chaos contract) — cross-reference if any
- CONTRACT-003 (Fizeau Service) — no change required (fizeau is below the worker; the API is between worker and ddx-server, not worker and fizeau)

NOT IN SCOPE FOR THIS ADR
- Specific code changes (this TD documents the design; implementation is follow-up beads from the roadmap)
- Multi-server federation (each server owns its workers; cross-server is a separate concern)
- Auth beyond session tokens (the worker-server channel is local; no remote workers in v1)
- Reshaping the bead schema (out of scope; ADR-004 owns that)

OUTPUTS (TD deliverables)
- ADR document at docs/helix/03-decide/ADR-XXX-worker-client-server-architecture.md
- Updated FEAT-006 (or proposed amendments listed)
- Sequence diagrams for: register, claim, execute, restart-recovery, graceful-shutdown
- Compatibility table: which existing tests/behaviors break, which migrate cleanly
- Implementation roadmap: ordered list of beads to file as follow-ups
- Overlap reconciliation table: each overlapping bead (above) classified as ABSORBED / CONFLICT-RESOLVED / PARALLEL-SAFE with explicit rationale

SEQUENCING DIRECTIVE (per user direction, 2026-05-03)
- Should land BEFORE major refactor children that touch the loop body (C5, C7, C9) — otherwise they'd refactor against the OLD model and need re-doing
- Should NOT block routine bug fixes or feature beads
- C5/C7/C9 explicitly FROZEN until the ADR's roadmap establishes the new package layout","events":[{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"resolved_provider":"claude","fallback_chain":[],"requested_harness":"claude"}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.009514865Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"attempt_id":"20260503T021424-dac100b6","harness":"claude","input_tokens":21,"output_tokens":14799,"total_tokens":14820,"cost_usd":1.0767455,"duration_ms":249728,"exit_code":0}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:38.0358447Z","kind":"cost","source":"ddx agent execute-bead","summary":"tokens=14820 cost_usd=1.0767"},{"actor":"ddx","body":"{"escalation_count":0,"fallback_chain":[],"final_tier":"","requested_profile":"","requested_tier":"","resolved_model":"","resolved_provider":"claude","resolved_tier":""}","created_at":"2026-05-03T02:18:43.263235583Z","kind":"routing","source":"ddx agent execute-loop","summary":"provider=claude"},{"actor":"erik","body":"APPROVE

2. (Outcome A only) Upstream fizeau change: file an issue/PR against github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/fizeau so that explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower triggers the routing engine within that harness's eligible models. For Class 2 harnesses (AutoRoutingEligible=false: gemini/opencode/pi), the engine returns an explicit "no auto-resolution available for harness=X" error, NOT silent empty Model.

3. (Outcome A only) Bump fizeau in cli/go.mod once the upstream change ships.

Integration test (in fizeau, or DDx-side stub if upstream test coverage is sufficient): TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness — assert ServiceExecuteRequest{Harness:"claude", Model:"", Profile:"code-economy"} → response RoutingActual.Model is non-empty AND is a member of the claude family per catalog.

5. Companion negative test: TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly — assert empty Model + empty Profile/MinPower returns a clear "under-specified routing" error instead of silently empty Model.

6. End-to-end verification (after cfedee8e/C9 also landed): ddx work --harness=claude on a small queue. Assert via:

Documentation: code comment on fizeau Execute call site (cli/internal/agent/service_run.go) noting that empty Model + Profile/MinPower is the well-formed request shape and what fizeau is expected to return.","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:06:53.276389609Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-1e516bc9","depends_on_id":"ddx-cfedee8e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:17:15Z"}],"description":"Upstream fizeau gap: when ServiceExecuteRequest carries an explicit Harness pin AND empty Model AND a non-empty Profile/MinPower, fizeau v0.10.3's Execute (service_execute.go:193-224) treats Harness as a hard pin and skips the routing engine entirely (it only invokes routing when req.Harness == ""). Result: request validates with empty Model, fizeau returns Model="", DDx passes no --model arg to the claude CLI, and Anthropic's CLI uses whatever the local user's session default is.

OBSERVED
- Active worker on ddx-23c67ca4 (PID 4065353): claude --print -p --verbose ... — no --model arg
- ddx server workers show worker-20260503T004125-0b67 → Model: empty, Harness: claude
- Operators cannot control which Claude model executes a bead via DDx routing for the claude harness; UI/spec.json reports Model="" (operator sees "claude" but not which Claude model)

SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY

This bead is HALF A of the original ddx-1e516bc9 problem. HALF B (DDx supplying Profile/MinPower in ServiceExecuteRequest) is owned by ddx-cfedee8e + C9 (ddx-9228a484), which wire the ladder into the executor closure and bump MinPower per each call. Without the ladder wiring, fizeau wouldn't have anything to route on even if this fix lands. With the ladder wiring AND this fizeau fix, the full path works.

INVESTIGATION FIRST
Confirm the gap before implementing. The hypothesis is that fizeau v0.10.3 service_execute.go:193-224 only runs routing when Harness == "". If that's wrong (e.g., fizeau already routes within harness when Profile/MinPower is supplied), this bead is a no-op and gets closed as already-resolved.

UPSTREAM CHANGE (if confirmed)
Modify fizeau's Execute so that when Harness is set and Model is empty and Profile or MinPower is non-empty, the routing engine runs WITHIN that harness's eligible models. Fizeau already has the catalog (models.yaml: opus-4.7 code-high :31-60; sonnet-4.6 code-medium :141-170; claude-haiku-5.5 code-economy :222-251) and harness eligibility (registry.go: claude/codex AutoRoutingEligible=true; gemini/opencode/pi false). The change is: skip the "Harness != '' means skip routing" gate when Model is empty AND a routing input (Profile/MinPower) is present.

For Class 2 harnesses (gemini/opencode/pi, AutoRoutingEligible=false): the engine should still surface a clear "no auto-resolution available" error — NOT silently return empty Model. DDx should treat that as a fail-fast on the attempt.

DDx-SIDE WORK (verification only — not implementation)
Once the ladder is wired (cfedee8e → C9), DDx will already submit Profile/MinPower for each Execute call. This bead's verification phase asserts that with cfedee8e landed AND fizeau bumped to the version with this fix, ddx work --harness=claude produces a non-empty resolved model in spec.json / result.json / server worker UI.

NOT IN SCOPE
- DDx-side ladder wiring or MinPower-supply changes (owned by ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-9228a484 C9)
- Operator-explicit --model passes (already work; pinned by operator intent)
- HTTP providers openrouter/lmstudio (no subprocess --model arg; not subprocess invocations)
- Stale resolveModel() in cli/internal/agent/runner.go:380 (legacy path; codex confirmed production uses RunWithConfigViaService → svc.Execute, not the legacy Run() path)

WHY P1 (was P0)
Demoted because:
- The DDx-side enabling work (cfedee8e/C9) is already P1/P2 in the queue
- This bead's effect is only observable after cfedee8e/C9 land
- It's an upstream fizeau bump, not a DDx code change
- Original P0 framing assumed a DDx-side fix in the dispatch path; that part is the refactor's job

DEPS
- ddx-cfedee8e (or its absorber ddx-9228a484 C9): the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on","id":"ddx-1e516bc9","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","kind:fix","upstream-fizeau","observed-failure"],"parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models (upstream gap)","updated_at":"2026-05-03T01:17:15.675058816Z"}

DEPS (gate execution; bead stays open until these close): refactor children C4-C13 (ddx-358f2457, ddx-c8f79963, ddx-b669bb9f, ddx-06eb05d8, ddx-848069a3, ddx-9228a484, ddx-0b8780db, ddx-c670ef0a, ddx-387a0178).","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:41.880567652Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-358f2457","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c8f79963","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-b669bb9f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-06eb05d8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-848069a3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-9228a484","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-0b8780db","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-c670ef0a","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"},{"issue_id":"ddx-31f745cd","depends_on_id":"ddx-387a0178","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-03T19:55:42Z"}],"description":"Hand-curated sub-agent prompts have been outperforming ddx work / ddx try in this session. Two competing hypotheses for why:
H1: ddx execution machinery (the 9 fail-prone layers) is unreliable — failure is in the workers/server code.
H2: Bead authoring quality is insufficient — beads punt investigation; hand-curation papers over the gap.

Both are plausible. Determining the relative contribution requires a 3-path study, NOT the original 2-path proposal.

THREE PATHS

Path A: ddx try / ddx work with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (current production path).
Path B: Sub-agent dispatched with the bead's auto-prompt verbatim (NOT hand-curated). Same prompt as A; different executor. Isolates ddx machinery from prompt quality.
Path C: Sub-agent dispatched with operator hand-curated prompt (file:line cites, named tests, explicit non-scope, etc.). Establishes the ceiling.

INTERPRETATION
- A vs B differ → ddx machinery is the failure (file follow-ups against the 9 layers).
- B vs C differ → bead authoring quality is the failure (already covered by bead-quality-audit + retrofit).
- A == B << C → both are real, with bead quality being the dominant gap.

METHOD
Pick 5 representative CLOSED beads as fixtures: one trivial fix, one refactor child, one test addition, one multi-file feature, one documentation update. For each fixture, run all 3 paths on a branch from the bead's base-rev (parent of the original work commit). Throwaway commits — the original work is already in main. We're measuring path properties, not producing mergeable code.

MEASUREMENTS
Per path per bead:
- Time-to-first-claim (A) / time-to-first-output (B,C)
- Time-to-merge-eligible-commit (whether or not we merge)
- Attempts (A) / iterations (B,C)
- Status outcomes: success / no_changes / preserved / blocked / errored
- Cost: tokens, USD
- Manual interventions
- Failure mode classified per the 9-layer machinery list

OUTPUT
.ddx/executions/<run-id>/ddx-vs-subagent-comparison-2026-05-03.md with:
- Per-bead per-path table
- Aggregate stats (median + p99)
- Failure-mode distribution
- Hypothesis verdict (H1 vs H2 vs both)
- Recommendations for which path is appropriate for which bead-shape

TIMING
This study runs AFTER the executeloop refactor (C0-C13) lands so the ddx machinery is its post-refactor shape. Running pre-refactor measures behavior we're about to change. RELIABILITY-PRINCIPLES + bead-quality-audit + retrofit + refactor must all complete first. This bead is filed now and EXECUTION DEFERRED.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Fixing whatever the study reveals (separate bug beads filed from the study's findings)
- Running against pre-refactor ddx machinery (data we won't act on)
- Comparing different sub-agent harnesses (different concern)

EVIDENCE
- ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome and is partial Path B-vs-C data favoring the bead-quality hypothesis.","id":"ddx-31f745cd","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","area:agent","area:tooling","kind:study","reliability","bead-quality"],"notes":"Partial Path B-vs-C evidence: ddx-f339c399 documents the 2026-05-04 ddx try ddx-29058e2a --harness=codex no_changes outcome, supporting the bead-quality hypothesis side of the study.","parent":"ddx-e34994e2","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"study: 3-path execution comparison (ddx work vs sub-agent + verbatim bead vs sub-agent + hand-curated)","updated_at":"2026-05-05T03:08:30.860761453Z"}

2. Detection at: cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go where ExecutionFailed is set (current lines 513 routing-preflight, 629/637 executor-error, 1011 in execute_bead.go) — each set point evaluates whether the failure is Disrupted.
3. The cooldown branch (lines 1074-1086) checks Disrupted FIRST: if Disrupted, skip SetExecutionCooldown entirely; just Unclaim so the bead returns to ready.
4. shouldSuppressNoProgress unchanged for genuine no-progress (BaseRev == ResultRev AND not Disrupted AND no rationale).
5. New diagnostic event `disruption_detected` with kind, reason, and bead context — emitted when Disrupted is set so operators can see disruption rates.
6. (Optional) Exponential backoff for non-Disrupted no-progress: first failure 5min; second 30min; third 2h; cap at 24h. Reset on success. If implementing: replace flat noProgressCooldown with this curve; document in code comment.
7. Tests:

   - TestLoop_GenuineNoProgress_StillCooldowns — model returns clean with no commit + rationale; assert 6h cooldown set
   - TestLoop_PreflightRejection_NoCooldown — routing preflight fails; assert disruption classification (operator config, not model failure)
   - TestLoop_DisruptionEventEmitted — assert disruption_detected event appears in bead events

Documentation: update FEAT-006 (or wherever the cooldown policy is documented) to describe Disrupted vs no-progress and the policy rationale.","claimed-at":"2026-05-03T02:17:24Z","claimed-machine":"eitri","claimed-pid":"163374","closing_commit_sha":"89473d084d436e925934549d31f560e5d6b8702a","created_at":"2026-05-03T01:47:21.609526243Z","description":"The current cooldown logic treats any ExecutionFailed status as evidence the model can't make progress, applying noProgressCooldown=6h when BaseRev == ResultRev (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1074-1086 via shouldSuppressNoProgress at line 1550). This silently freezes important work whenever a worker is disrupted (killed, server restart, network blip, OS issue) — disruption produces ExecutionFailed + no commit, indistinguishable from "model genuinely tried and produced nothing."

OBSERVED (today, 2026-05-02)
After the picker fix investigation found the four "starved" P0 beads (ddx-dc157075, ddx-aee651ec, ddx-29058e2a, ddx-9d55601f) all had execute-loop-retry-after timestamps ~3 hours in the future. Investigation per ddx-9d55601f confirmed these were NOT genuine model failures — they were prior worker disruptions during this development session (worker kills, server restarts) being treated as model-gave-up.

User directive: "Cooldown should be reserved for cases where the model is truly unable to make progress on a task."

CURRENT COOLDOWN POLICY (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go)
- LandConflictCooldown = 15min — short cooldown after merge/land conflicts (line 64)
- noProgressCooldown = 6h default — when shouldSuppressNoProgress is true (line 367)
- MaxLoopCooldown = 24h — push_failed, push_conflict, decomposition decline (line 57)

shouldSuppressNoProgress (line 1550): returns true if BaseRev != "" AND ResultRev != "" AND BaseRev == ResultRev. This catches:
- Model wrote nothing AND came back cleanly (true no_progress) ✓
- Worker killed mid-execution before commit (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Network/provider error before model started (FALSE no_progress — disruption)
- Routing preflight rejection (FALSE no_progress — config issue)

The mis-classification is the bug.

PROPOSED FIX

Add a Disposition::Disrupted variant (or equivalent flag on ExecuteBeadReport) that explicitly marks results that are NOT evidence of model inability:
- ctx.Err() != nil (cancellation, deadline, parent died)
- syscall.SIGTERM / SIGINT received during execution
- Provider-side 5xx, connection refused, timeout (transport disruption)
- Worker process killed (parent never received clean result)
- Server-restart-detected marker
- Routing preflight rejection (operator config issue, not model failure)

For Disrupted: skip cooldown entirely (or use a tiny defer like 30s) and unclaim the bead so the next worker pickup is immediate.
For genuine no-progress (model returned clean with no commit AND rationale present): keep current 6h cooldown.

ALTERNATIVE: exponential backoff per bead. First failure: 5min; second: 30min; third: 2h; cap at 24h. Resets on any successful attempt or operator clear. This is more forgiving than the current flat 6h on the first hiccup.

PROBABLY BOTH: Disrupted skips cooldown entirely; non-Disrupted failures use exponential backoff starting at 5min, not flat 6h.

DETECTION POINTERS

- ctx.Err() check at the call site that produces ExecutionFailed (line 513, 629, 637, 1011)
- Check for `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/no_changes_rationale.txt` presence — if missing, the model never wrote one (likely disruption)
- Check the executor's exit code: SIGKILL/SIGTERM = disruption; clean exit with empty result = no-progress
- Track parent process restart count or boot ID; if changed since the attempt started, mark Disrupted

NOT IN SCOPE
- Changing the cooldown duration constants themselves
- Reworking the per-bead retry policy framework (separate concern)
- Changing land-conflict or push-failed cooldowns (those are correct as-is)
- The exponential-backoff alternative is OPTIONAL; primary fix is Disrupted classification

INTERSECTION WITH OTHER BEADS
- ddx-dc157075 (worker stay-alive): the stay-alive fix prevents some classes of disruption (worker no longer exits on empty poll), but does NOT fix the cooldown-on-disruption mis-classification
- ddx-076147ee (worker client-server architecture TD): if workers become long-lived clients, server-restart disruption goes away by construction. But this fix is needed BEFORE that TD is implemented because the disruption classification is needed regardless.
- ddx-9228a484 C9 (StopCondition enum): may absorb this — the StopCondition enum could include Disrupted as a state. Execute-loop cost-cap and stop logic is scattered across executor closure in cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go (lines 1722-1730, 1818-1850) and ad-hoc loop continuation checks. Stopping conditions (Drained, Once, Signal, Budget, NoProgress, Blocked) are evaluated implicitly with no enum, making the stop logic brittle and hard to extend. The escalation ladder (escalation.Ladder.Next) exists in cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go but has no production caller — dead-code audit (ddx-09d2990c) must confirm before this child can safely wire or delete it.

ROOT CAUSE
- Cost cap: cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1722-1730 checks max_cost inside executor closure; cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1818-1850 has a second cost-cap site. Neither site uses a StopCondition type.
- Escalation: cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30 defines Ladder.Next; no call site in production confirmed (dead-code audit required per ddx-09d2990c).
- Reviewer cost (from ddx-a8718bec): reviewer cost is tracked in a separate accumulator inside drain but not evaluated in a unified stop gate.
- Worker stay-alive tactical (from ddx-dc157075): poll-interval default is 0 (drain mode); the tactical default of 30s should come from C10 (ddx-f9acb86d) but cost-cap relocation must happen first.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/stop.go defining StopCondition enum: Drained | Once | Signal | Budget | NoProgress | Blocked.
- Add drain.stop(state DrainState) StopCondition — evaluates all stop conditions including cost cap.
- Remove cost cap from executor closure (agent_cmd.go:1722-1730, 1818-1850); move into drain.stop budget evaluation.
- Relocate ddx-cfedee8e MinPower-ladder behavior into try.Attempt Retry path (wire Ladder.Next or confirm deletion from dead-code audit).
- Relocate ddx-a8718bec reviewer cost accumulator into drain.stop budget evaluation.
- Subsume ddx-dc157075 tactical changes.
- After cost-cap consumers audit: find all dashboard/alerting consumers of execution_failed with max-cost reason; document in .ddx/executions/<run-id>/cost-cap-consumers.md.

NON-SCOPE
- Poll-interval default (30s) belongs to C10 (ddx-f9acb86d).
- Frontend changes.
- Removal of ddx-cfedee8e / ddx-a8718bec / ddx-dc157075 beads (operator closes those after confirming subsumption).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: stop conditions should fail open into a bounded stop state, not a worker wedge.
- P2: StopCondition owns stop classification, not unrelated eligibility or routing policy.
- P3: budget and max-cost transitions should be observable.
- P4: cost-cap behavior must stay local to the affected run.
- P5: the worker state should remain visible while it is claiming, executing, or blocked.
- P6: cooldown must stay limited to transient no-progress classes.","id":"ddx-9228a484","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"C9: first-class StopCondition enum + cost-cap relocation (absorbs ddx-cfedee8e + ddx-a8718bec + ddx-dc157075 behavior)","updated_at":"2026-05-05T07:43:37.227044785Z"}

Phase events (queueing, running, terminal, bead-result JSONL) are emitted from 4 separate sites in execute_bead_loop.go. Adding a new phase or changing event shape requires coordinated edits across all 4 sites, introducing subtle divergence. Today the JSONL-on-disk shape is only verified by manual inspection against a baseline fixture.

ROOT CAUSE
The 4 emission sites in cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:
- :577 — queueing phase event
- :596 — running phase event
- :1076-1091 — terminal phase event (with Disposition/ParkReason)
- :1093 — bead.result JSONL write

Each site constructs and emits events independently with no shared Phase enum or emitPhase abstraction. The C0 fixture diff approach (byte-identical JSONL baseline) is the only correctness gate, but it is not automated.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add cli/internal/agent/work/phases.go defining Phase enum: Queueing | Running | Terminal.
- Add emitPhase(ctx, store, beadID string, phase Phase, outcome Outcome) error called by state transitions.
- Replace the 4 current emission sites with calls to emitPhase.
- Verify JSONL on-disk shape is byte-identical to the C0 baseline (automated fixture diff in test).

NON-SCOPE
- Changing the JSONL event format or adding new event types.
- Renaming execute_bead_loop.go (separate bead ddx-1d867ec1).

INTERSECTIONS
- P1: terminal phase emission should fail open rather than silently diverge across call sites.
- P3: the emitted phase stream must stay observable and structured.
- P4: a phase-event bug on one bead must not affect other beads.
- P5: the phase stream supports operator-visible state.","id":"ddx-c670ef0a","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:refactor"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"C12: single phase-event emission (replaces 4 emit sites with state-machine-driven emitPhase)","updated_at":"2026-05-05T07:43:38.001537821Z"}

Each bead currently gets exactly one singleTierAttempt without any rung escalation on substantive failure. The existing tier→profile mutation in the loop is an ad-hoc workaround that should be replaced by the ladder.

ROOT CAUSE

- cli/internal/escalation/escalation.go:30: Ladder.Next is defined but has no call site in execute_bead_loop.go or any other production file (confirmed by dead-code audit ddx-09d2990c).

PROPOSED FIX

- Remove the existing tier→profile mutation; ladder.Next drives all MinPower escalation.
- Pass Role='implementer' and CorrelationID='<bead_id>:<attempt_id>' on every ExecuteRequest.

- Changing the ladder definition or rung configuration (S10_2 owns that).

The runs/[runId] page is a single-panel flat view with no tab navigation, no URL-driven tab state, and no separation between Overview, Prompt, Response, Tools, and Session data. Users cannot deep-link to a specific view. Shared sub-components between the runs/[runId] and executions/[executionId] pages are duplicated.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte is a monolithic flat page without tab structure.
- Subcomponents for prompt/response/tools/session panels are inlined or missing; no lib/runDetail/ shared library exists.
- URL query parameter ?tab= is not implemented; tab state is not preserved on navigation.
- Depends on ddx-9228a484 (C9) to stabilize the StopCondition / run record shape before building the Session tab.

PROPOSED FIX
- Promote shared subcomponents from cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/executions/[executionId]/+page.svelte into cli/internal/server/frontend/src/lib/runDetail/ (tab shell, prompt panel, response panel, tools panel, session panel).
- Rebuild cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/runs/[runId]/+page.svelte as tabbed: Overview / Prompt / Response / Tools / Session.
- Implement URL-driven tab state via ?tab=<name> query parameter; deep-link preserves tab on refresh.
- Overview tab: status + last-update timestamp + run/try/work layer chip + base/result rev + link to worker page for running runs.
- Tools tab: paginated 50/page with Load more; lazy-loads on activation.
- Session tab: tokens (prompt/completion/total/cached), billingMode, base/result revs, stderr/stderrPath link.

NON-SCOPE
- Evidence tab (ddx-4cd64068, a child bead).
- New subscription infrastructure (reuse existing workerProgress).","id":"ddx-f04faa86","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","story:16"],"parent":"ddx-a8a18cb7","priority":2,"status":"open","title":"Story 16.1: Frontend rebuild runs/[runId]/+page.svelte as tabbed run-detail page","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:10:12.835283429Z"}

4. Default poll_interval = 30s in cli/internal/server/server.go.
5. --once flag still exits on empty queue.
6. Explicit --poll-interval=0 still exits on empty queue (legacy drain mode).
7. Server-managed workers: no_ready_work mapped to running/idle substate (not exited).
8. ddx-dc157075 closed as superseded with pointer to this bead.
9. TestDrain_DefaultPollInterval_30s verifies default is 30s when no flag set.

12. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28.498901669Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-f9acb86d","depends_on_id":"ddx-9228a484","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-05-02T20:02:28Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
ddx work defaults to --poll-interval=0 (drain mode), which exits as soon as the ready queue is empty. Workers running in a long-lived server context terminate on first empty-queue poll instead of staying alive for the next bead. This makes server-managed workers non-viable for persistent operation.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/cmd/work.go: default --poll-interval flag is 0 (or absent); drain mode exits on empty queue.
- cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go: GraphQL worker dispatch also defaults to poll_interval=0.
- cli/internal/server/server.go: server-spawned workers use same default.
- cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go: execute-loop base command also has poll_interval default at 0.
- No_ready_work state is treated as a terminal exit (not a transient idle substate) in server-managed workers.
ddx-dc157075 proposed a 30s default tactically; this child (C10) subsumes it fully after C9 (ddx-9228a484) lands.

PROPOSED FIX
- Change default --poll-interval to 30s in all four sites: cli/cmd/work.go, cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go, cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go, cli/internal/server/server.go.
- Preserve --once exit-on-empty behavior.
- Preserve explicit --poll-interval=0 as legacy drain mode (backward compat).
- Map server-managed workers' no_ready_work to running/idle substate (transient) instead of exited (terminal).
- Close ddx-dc157075 as superseded after this lands.

NON-SCOPE
- StopCondition enum (belongs to C9 / ddx-9228a484, a dep).
- Frontend changes to worker display.","id":"ddx-f9acb86d","issue_type":"task","labels":["phase:2","refactor","kind:feature"],"parent":"ddx-5cb6e6cd","priority":1,"status":"open","title":"C10: ddx work stay-alive default + transient idle substate (subsumes ddx-dc157075 fully)","updated_at":"2026-05-04T16:08:52.41207664Z"}
